description: Per-lead vote on architect-proposed global rules during the rules_aggregate stage. Single-veto applies — any reject from any active role's lead bars the global from binding this iteration. The lead checks fit with the role's stack and reality, drift against root-promoted rules, and coherence against the role's own pending rules from rules_lead.

rules.description: One vote per pending architect global. Vote on every entry; skipping is not a vote.

id.description: The pending global's id, copied verbatim.

reason.description: Required when verdict=reject. One short line naming the concrete conflict — cite the conflicting root rule id, your role's pending rule id, or the stack reality that prevents honoring the global.

examples.title: Mixed verdicts — accepts most, rejects one for drift, one for stack reality, one for incomplete promotion

rules.reason: Conflicts with root rule v84-0.global.rule.4 — that rule requires UUIDv7 ids project-wide, the proposed global mandates CUIDs.
Backend uses an async job queue by design; a 'all writes are synchronous' global cannot be honored without rewriting the queue layer.
promotes_from cites v84-1.backend.lead.rule.7 (logging context shape), but the global only describes log levels — it does not cover the structured context fields the lead rule mandates.

examples.title: All accepts — clean run with no conflicts
